page.title: Sotogrande Polo Final 2026: Best Marbella Yacht Charter Packages

data.meta_description: Planning a Marbella yacht charter for the Sotogrande Polo Final 2026? Cruise 20NM of coast from €749/2h, then celebrate the Copa de Oro final. Book now.
data.summary: For the 2026 Sotogrande Polo Final, guests base themselves in Marbella and charter a boat from Puerto Banús for a pre-match coastal cruise or a post-final sunset celebration. Packages run from the two-hour Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 (€749) up to the Mangusta 80 (€4,719, 4h minimum), with skipper, drinks and VAT included on every booking.
key_facts: Copa de Oro final traditionally falls on the last Sunday of August at Santa María Polo Club, Sotogrande
Puerto Banús to the Sotogrande coastline is roughly 20 nautical miles by sea
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749 for 2h, up to €2,299 for 8h, skipper and drinks included
Mangusta 80 (white or grey): €4,719 minimum 4 hours, jet ski included free

August water temperatures off Marbella typically sit around 23-24°C
Polo final weekend books out fast — most guests reserve their boat 3-4 weeks ahead

faq.q: Can your boats sail into Sotogrande Marina for the polo final?
faq.a: Our fleet operates out of Puerto Banús rather than Sotogrande Marina, roughly 20 nautical miles west. Most guests use the boat for a coastal cruise before or after the match — sunbathing, swimming stops, drinks on board — then take a taxi the short distance inland to Santa María Polo Club for the final itself.

faq.q: What's the best way to combine a yacht charter with the Polo Final?
faq.a: Book a morning or early-afternoon slot so you're back at Puerto Banús with time to reach the polo grounds before the match starts, or reverse it: watch the final, then head straight to the marina for a sunset charter and cava toast to round off the day.

faq.q: How much does a yacht charter cost during polo final week?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for two hours, rising to €2,299 for a full eight-hour day. The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a four-hour minimum. Pricing for the rest of the fleet is on request — message us on WhatsApp with your group size and dates.

faq.q: How far in advance should we book for the Polo Final weekend?
faq.a: Book 3-4 weeks ahead if possible. Late August is peak season in Marbella and the polo final draws extra demand for boats along this stretch of coast, so the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 and Mangusta 80 slots fill first.

faq.q: How big a group can charter a boat for the day?
faq.a: It depends on the boat. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 each take up to 9 and 11 guests respectively, while the Mangusta 80 carries up to 12. For larger polo parties, check our full fleet for other 10-12 pax options such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Sunseeker 68 Sport.

faq.q: Is alcohol included if we want to celebrate the final on board?
faq.a: Yes. Every charter includes beer, white wine, cava and soft drinks as standard, plus light snacks. If your group wants to mark a polo win with a proper toast, cava is already on board — no extra ordering needed.

faq.q: What's the weather like off Marbella in late August?
faq.a: Expect sea temperatures around 23-24°C, light afternoon breezes and long daylight hours well into the evening — ideal for a post-final sunset cruise. See our full August weather guide for planning details.

faq.q: Can we book a boat for a corporate or sponsor group during polo week?
faq.a: Yes. Several charters double as hospitality for sponsors and clients visiting for the polo, particularly the larger boats with 10-12 capacity. See our corporate boat hire page for group booking details and invoicing.

data.body_html:
The Sotogrande Polo Final 2026 — the Copa de Oro decider at Santa María Polo Club, traditionally staged on the last Sunday of August — pulls a crowd of spectators, sponsors and players along this stretch of Andalucian coast. A growing number of them are basing themselves in Marbella and pairing the final with a yacht charter from Puerto Banús, roughly 20 nautical miles up the coast. This guide covers how to structure the day, what it costs, and which boats in our fleet suit a polo-weekend charter.

Why pair a Marbella yacht charter with the Polo Final

Sotogrande fills up fast on final weekend — traffic on the AP-7 backs up, and marina berths and beach clubs get booked out months in advance. Basing yourself in Marbella instead gives you more charter choice and a shorter final approach: Puerto Banús to the Sotogrande coastline is about 20 nautical miles, roughly an hour by sea depending on the boat. Guests use that window for open-water swimming, a lunch stop in a quiet cala, or simply cruising the coastline past the Golden Mile and Río Verde before heading inland to the polo grounds.

Boats that suit the polo final crowd

Group size and mood should drive the choice. For a tighter group wanting a fast, sporty half-day before the match, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) both depart Puerto Banús and cover the coastal run comfortably. For a full-day celebration built around the final — arriving by boat, watching from the grounds, then returning to a sunset spread — the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) has the deck space and shade for a longer, more relaxed charter, plus a jet ski included at no extra cost. Larger sponsor or family groups can ask about the Azimut 58 Flybridge, Sunseeker 68 Sport or Ferretti 94, all 10-12 pax boats priced on request.

A sample polo final day on the water

Most guests run one of two structures. Option one: board at Puerto Banús mid-morning, cruise east past Marbella Marina and Cabopino, swim and lunch on board, then dock back in time to reach Santa María Polo Club for the afternoon final by taxi. Option two: watch the final first, then head to the marina for a late-afternoon or sunset charter — cava on board, no crowds, and the Golden Mile lit up as the sun goes down. Either way, build in at least 90 minutes of slack either side of the sailing window, since polo weekend traffic on the coast road is heavier than usual.

Pricing for polo final week

Our pricing doesn't change for peak dates, but availability does — book earlier than you normally would. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for a 2-hour charter, rising to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day; both figures include skipper, drinks and Spanish IVA at 21%. The Mangusta 80, in white or grey, starts at €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum, with a jet ski included free of charge. Every other boat in the fleet — from the compact Dubhe to the 29m Mefasa 90 — is priced on request; message us on WhatsApp with your dates, group size and preferred boat and we'll confirm availability and cost.

What's included on every charter

  • Licensed skipper for the full duration of the charter
  • Fuel and insurance
  • Soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava on board
  • Light snacks
  • Full safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) already factored into the price

Nothing extra to arrange before the final — the drinks for a celebratory toast are already stocked, whether you're marking a win on the pitch or just a good day on the water. For a broader look at what a day charter covers, see our day charter guide.

Best time to book

Late August is already peak season on this coast, and the polo final adds extra demand for boats specifically, so the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 and Mangusta 80 tend to book out 3-4 weeks ahead of the final. Sea conditions in late August are reliably calm, with water around 23-24°C and long evening light — good for either a morning cruise or a sunset return leg. More detail on conditions is in our August weather guide.

Corporate and sponsor group charters

Polo weekend brings sponsor hospitality and client entertaining alongside the spectator crowd. Several of our larger boats work well for this — enough deck space for 10-12 guests, a skipper who can hold a fixed schedule around a match time, and drinks included so there's nothing to invoice separately. See our corporate boat hire page for group rates and booking terms, or start with the full Boat Rental Marbella fleet to compare every option departing Puerto Banús.

Frequently asked questions

Can your boats sail into Sotogrande Marina for the polo final?

Our fleet operates out of Puerto Banús rather than Sotogrande Marina, roughly 20 nautical miles west. Most guests use the boat for a coastal cruise before or after the match — sunbathing, swimming stops, drinks on board — then take a taxi the short distance inland to Santa María Polo Club for the final itself.

What's the best way to combine a yacht charter with the Polo Final?

Book a morning or early-afternoon slot so you're back at Puerto Banús with time to reach the polo grounds before the match starts, or reverse it: watch the final, then head straight to the marina for a sunset charter and cava toast to round off the day.

How much does a yacht charter cost during polo final week?

The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for two hours, rising to €2,299 for a full eight-hour day. The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a four-hour minimum. Pricing for the rest of the fleet is on request — message us on WhatsApp with your group size and dates.

How far in advance should we book for the Polo Final weekend?

Book 3-4 weeks ahead if possible. Late August is peak season in Marbella and the polo final draws extra demand for boats along this stretch of coast, so the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 and Mangusta 80 slots fill first.

How big a group can charter a boat for the day?

It depends on the boat. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 each take up to 9 and 11 guests respectively, while the Mangusta 80 carries up to 12. For larger polo parties, check our full fleet for other 10-12 pax options such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Sunseeker 68 Sport.

Is alcohol included if we want to celebrate the final on board?

Yes. Every charter includes beer, white wine, cava and soft drinks as standard, plus light snacks. If your group wants to mark a polo win with a proper toast, cava is already on board — no extra ordering needed.

What's the weather like off Marbella in late August?

Expect sea temperatures around 23-24°C, light afternoon breezes and long daylight hours well into the evening — ideal for a post-final sunset cruise. See our full breakdown on the August weather guide for planning details.

Can we book a boat for a corporate or sponsor group during polo week?

Yes. Several charters double as hospitality for sponsors and clients visiting for the polo, particularly the larger boats with 10-12 capacity. See our corporate boat hire page for group booking details and invoicing.
